regimeSub: Reflationary regime: 4.2% CPI and a Warsh-led Fed signaling hikes, even as the Iran war de-escalates and oil unwinds its spike.
themes: Hormuz reopens + Saudi price cuts -> Brent back to ~$73 prewar, removing the oil tail risk
CPI at 4.2% (3-yr high) -> Fed dot plot pivots hawkish, September hike live despite cooling oil
Negative US real policy rate -> gold pinned near $4,086 as inflation hedge stays bid
OpenAI IPO flop + tech vol -> AI/momentum unwind threatens narrow leadership even as SPX prints 7,354

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63% vs CPI 4.2% = negative real rate; Fed behind the curve, hike bias justified.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +31bp, modestly steep; market prices hikes but doubts the Fed kills growth—no recession signal.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Tight with VIX at 18; credit ignoring tech wobble and hike risk—complacent, fade strength.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 18.4, off the war highs; geopolitical premium drained but tech vol keeps a floor under it.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $73, fully round-tripped to prewar as Hormuz reopens; oil now a disinflationary tailwind.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4,086, holding near highs on negative real rates and structural debasement bid—stay long.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1,544 weak won despite soft DXY; KRW pressured by tech/AI rotation, watch KOSPI 8,244 froth.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: AI trade cracking post-OpenAI IPO blow; the $7trn energy/AI narrative is the market's fault line.

headline: Oil has round-tripped its Iran-war spike as Hormuz reopens, but 4.2% inflation and a hawkish Warsh Fed leave reflation and a September hike on the table, with a fragile AI trade the key risk.
